rulings.Case Title: ANTHONY PONTINO VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 19, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Boss Manufacturing Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its burden of production and plaintiff failed to present admissible evidence creating a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to plaintiff¿s declaration are sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 10:15am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: GLENN CRESSY VS. HOPEMAN BROTHERS, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JANUARY 19, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 7, Brayton Purcell¿s motion to be relieved as counsel for plaintiff Glenn Cressy is denied. Brayton Purcell failed to provide proper notice pursuant to C.C.P. 1005(b), which increases the 16-court-day notice period by 20 days when service is by mail to a location outside the United States. The proof of service demonstrates that Brayton Purcell served plaintiff on December 22, 2011 by office mailing to an address in Ecuador. Although an email address is listed, there is no authority allowing for service by email. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 10:15am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If this tentative ruling is contested, Brayton Purcell is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing and must also prepare a proposed order drafted in Brayton Purcell¿s favor and bring that to the hearing. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: OSCAR BONETTO, ET AL VS. DOWMAN PRODUCTS, INC.
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JANUARY 19, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 8, Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Co., Inc.¿s amended motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs¿ claim for punitive damages is denied. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiffs submitted sufficient evidence to create a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Defendant¿s objections to Dr. Cohen¿s declaration are overruled. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Crum¿s deposition testimony are overruled. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Crosby¿s deposition testimony are overruled. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 10:15am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: LARRY CAMPBELL VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Dismiss Action For Delay In Prosecution
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JANUARY 19, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 9, Defendant Ford Motor Co.¿s motion to dismiss for delay in prosecution pursuant to C.C.P. 583.310 and 583.360 is denied. Defendant represented that plaintiff is in propia persona. Defendant failed to provide proper notice pursuant to C.C.P. 1005(b), which increases the 16-court-day notice period by 10 days when service is by mail to a location outside of California but within the United States. The proof of service demonstrates that defendant served plaintiff by mail on December 19, 2011 to an address in Nevada. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 10:15am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If this tentative ruling is contested, defendant is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing and must also prepare a proposed order drafted in defendant¿s favor and bring that to the hearing. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: GEORGE MC NAMARA VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Dismiss Action For Delay In Prosecution
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JANUARY 19, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 10, Defendant Ford Motor Co.¿s motion to dismiss for delay in prosecution pursuant to C.C.P. 583.310 and 583.360 is denied. Defendant represented that plaintiff is in propia persona. Defendant failed to provide proper notice pursuant to C.C.P. 1005(b), which increases the 16-court-day notice period by 5 days when service is by mail to a location within California. The proof of service demonstrates that defendant served plaintiff on December 19, 2011 by office mailing to an address in California. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 10:15am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If this tentative ruling is contested, defendant is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing and must also prepare a proposed order drafted in defendant¿s favor and bring that to the hearing. = (503/TLJ)
